search_query: man jailed as teen for decades paroled
combined_text: Paul Geidel "From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedianavigation search Paul Geidel Jr. (April 21, 1894 – May 1, 1987) was the longest-serving prison inmate in the United States, whose sentence ended with his parole. After being convicted of second-degree murder in 1911, aged 17, Geidel served 68 years and 245 days
